events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant", "installed-defective"],
  "percent_complete": 70,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Flex duct sagging more than 1/2 inch per foot of length, exceeding SMACNA and Manual D support requirements",
    "Duct connections not sealed with approved mastic or UL 181 tape, violating IMC 603.9",
    "Flex duct compressed and kinked at several bends, reducing airflow below Manual D design values",
    "Support straps cutting into flex duct insulation, causing potential thermal bypass and damage"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install additional supports to eliminate sagging, spaced per manufacturer and SMACNA standards",
    "Seal all duct connections and seams with UL 181 listed mastic or tape",
    "Replace kinked or compressed flex duct sections and ensure bends have adequate radius",
    "Replace damaged support straps with wide, non-cutting hangers and protect insulation"
  ],
  "rationale": "The duct installation exhibits multiple code and standard violations that compromise system performance and safety. Sagging, unsealed connections, and kinked flex duct reduce airflow, increase leakage, and can lead to condensation and IAQ issues. These defects must be corrected before the system can be accepted.",
  "references": [
    "IMC 603.9 - Duct and plenum sealing",
    "SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards - Flexible Duct Installation",
    "Manual D - Duct design and support requirements"
  ]
}
